title: Less Grain Exports Expected Next Season
section_path: Less Grain Exports Expected Next Season
section_path_text: Less Grain Exports Expected Next Season
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
As we discussed in Commodore Research's most recent Weekly Executive Report, The United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) now predicts that global coarse grain, wheat, soybean, and soymeal exports will total 739.3 million tons in the upcoming 2026/27 season. This is 23.2 million tons (-3%) less than expected for the current 2025/26 season. Overall, this is a firm amount (and the forecast, of course, remains preliminary and will change as the months progress).
